title: CLIA RPG Phase 1 — SwiftUI read-only views over typed substrate records (Trainer Panel + Pokémon Roster + Role Detail + Move List + Tool Bag + Pokédex)
description:
Per CLIA RPG product vision landed 2026-05-31 at kura-spaces/substrate-game/v0.1.0/clia-rpg-product-vision-trainer-role-power-tool.md, Phase 1 is the smallest viable demo: 6 read-only SwiftUI screens consuming typed JSON via Codable that render the substrate as a Pokémon-style UI. The substrate ALREADY has typed records for every screen — no new schemas needed, just SwiftUI views.

## Phase 1 Screens (6 total)

1. **Trainer Panel** — agent identity + party (assigned roles) + badges (gates cleared) + Pokédex completion %. Data source: agents/<slug>/private/universal/identity/ + chronicle techo + axiom citations.

2. **Pokémon Roster** — card-list of agent's assigned roles. Each card shows role name + role-class type (color-coded per contribution-type) + level (axiom citations) + HP/MP bars (context/attention budget). Data source: substrate/roles/<slug>/role-surface-manifest.json + assignment records.

3. **Role Detail Screen** — full surface for one role. Shows role description + acting-framing + contribution-mix (pie chart) + Move List (workflows the role can fire) + Tool inventory (digikoma refs) + status effects + held items + evolution criteria. Data source: role-surface-manifest.json + role-workflow.role-workflow.json + tool kura space lookups.

4. **Move List** — per-role list of workflows as moves. Columns: Move name | Type (contribution-type) | Power (typed-records produced) | Accuracy (build-test-validation pass rate) | PP/Tools (required tools list with availability). Data source: each role's role-workflow stages + tool-exclusivity registry + minimization metrics.

5. **Tool Bag** — inventory grid of typed tools the agent has access to. Each tool shows slug + name + kind (cli/filesystem-write/digikoma/typed-record-walker) + availability status + used-by-roles list. Data source: kura-spaces/tools/v0.1.0/<slug>.tool.json.

6. **Pokédex Screen** — browse/search the substrate-typed catalog. Tabs: Agents, Roles, Workflows, Tools, Contribution-types, Axioms, Workstreams, Encounter types. Each entry shows typed description + relationships + evolution criteria + Pokédex completion status. Data source: the substrate's typed-record kura space + agent's chronicle/journal/expertise lanes.

## Implementation Approach

- **SwiftUI app** at a new package home (likely substrate/collectives/clia-app-org/private/apple/apps/clia-rpg/ following the same shape as clia-roles app at clia-app-org)
- **Codable structs** mirroring the typed-record JSON shapes (RoleSurfaceManifestModel, ToolRecord, WorkflowRecord, etc.)
- **Async file readers** that walk substrate paths and decode typed records on-demand
- **NavigationStack** wiring the 6 screens (Trainer Panel → Pokémon Roster → Role Detail → Move List | Tool Bag | Pokédex)
- **No state machine logic yet** — just typed-record visualization. Phase 2 adds live state.
- Use **ModernSharedAppShell** + **wrkstrm-components** UI primitives per substrate convention
- Use **WrkstrmColor** + **CommonDesignTokenService** for color/spacing
- Contribution-type color coding: future-pointing — type chart authoring is a separate bead

## Estimated Scope

~15-20 hours focused implementation. Roughly 2-3 SwiftUI files per screen × 6 screens = ~12-18 files. Plus Codable models + file readers + navigation wiring. The substrate's typed-everything investment pays off here — most work is rendering, not modeling.

## Composition With Existing Substrate

- **Reads from** every typed record the substrate has: agent identities, roles, role-workflows, workflows, tools, axioms, workstreams, contribution-types, etc.
- **Does NOT write back** — Phase 1 is read-only. Phase 3 adds action surfaces (Pokéball capture invoking capture-state, etc.)
- **Composes with bead [[cascade-skill-implementation]]** — Phase 3's action surfaces would invoke cascade-task-creation + cascade-execution through this UI
- **Composes with bead [[workflow-invoker-skill]]** — Phase 3's move-selection invokes workflow-invoker
- **Composes with bead [[unknown-workflow-skill-implementation]]** — Phase 2's encounter classification triggers unknown-workflow
- **Pre-requisite for spawn-software handoff**: a CLIA RPG spawn-request-packet could carry this Phase 1 scope as its `softwareSummary` for a future spawn-software walk

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 6 screens render with real data from substrate-typed records
- Operator can navigate Trainer Panel → Pokémon Roster → Role Detail without crashes
- Move List renders workflows as moves with correct type/power/accuracy/tools mapping
- Tool Bag accurately reflects kura-spaces/tools/v0.1.0/ contents
- Pokédex search/browse works across all 8 tabs
- 0 hand-coded typed-data — every screen reads from substrate JSON
- xcodebuild succeeds; SwiftUI previews render

## Pre-Spawn Recommendation

This bead is SUBSTANTIAL enough that it warrants its own spawn-software workstream walk per substrate's tool-authoring-runs-spawn-software-workstream axiom. Recommended sequence: (1) operator approves Phase 1 scope inline → (2) author spawn-request-packet for `clia-rpg-phase-1` at spawn-software/instances/ → (3) walk the 11 typed gates (ontology-review, artifact-plan, design-truth, etc.) → (4) ship. Bypassing spawn-software for substantial app work risks the same doctrinal violation as md@swift-universal.cli earlier this session.

Originally surfaced 2026-05-31 by operator after CLIA RPG product vision landed: 'bead-track the Phase 1 SwiftUI implementation'.
